articles.title: FRAMEWORK + SCORECARD: AI PROTOTYPING FOR PRODUCT LEADERS
articles.summary: According to Forrester, 88% of engineering, product, and design leaders think prototyping needs to happen earlier. Wait til late in the lifecycle to surface changes and you'll get months of delays.

articles.title: 3️⃣ Tooling evaluation framework + scorecard
articles.summary: Tackle problems early in the lifecycle by accelerating your

articles.title: prototyping strategy. Get the guide to learn how.
THE LINE BETWEEN AI-FIRST AND AI THEATER
articles.summary: AI-first work fails when it stops at impressive demos. Real value comes from product ownership, clear outcomes, and responsibility once AI touches the product.

articles.title: WHY EVERY EXEC SHOULD BE VIBECODING
articles.summary: Vibecoding keeps executives close to the work. By building with AI tools themselves, leaders can move faster, align teams better, and turn ideas into impact without layers of process.

articles.title: AI'S MISSING INGREDIENT
articles.summary: AI will not win through speed alone. The companies that succeed will be the ones that use design to turn raw capability into products humans actually understand and care about.

articles.title: MY TIME AT AMAZON, PART I
articles.summary: Big Tech turns ambition into a constant state of striving that rarely satisfies. Status feels validating at first, but the cost to identity, family, and peace becomes impossible to ignore.

articles.title: LIFE OF A BIRB
articles.summary: Finch's success comes from designing onboarding and monetization around fast engagement and habit formation. By removing friction and guiding users to an early payoff, the app turns curiosity into retention.

articles.title: WHAT MAKES GENERATED UI WORTH KEEPING?
articles.summary: AI-generated UI only becomes valuable when it reflects real product constraints. Designing with brand, data, and existing patterns from the start turns AI output into something teams can actually build on.

articles.title: I COULDN'T ADAPT TO BARCELONA'S MEAL SCHEDULE FOR THE SAME REASON YOUR PROCESS CHANGES AREN'T LANDING
articles.summary: Culture change does not fail because people are confused. It fails because beliefs cannot shift without lived experience and the right context.

articles.title: UX STRATEGIST: THE ONLY JOB WHERE SAYING ‘IT DEPENDS' IS CONSIDERED EXPERTISE
articles.summary: “It depends” is often used by UX strategists to avoid making decisions. This avoidance slows teams down and keeps real product problems from being fixed.

articles.title: DESIGN TOOLS ARE THE NEW DESIGN DELIVERABLES
articles.summary: Design deliverables are shifting from static assets to custom tools.

articles.title: AVOID “ASAP” AND OTHER HIGH-STRUNG, NON-SPECIFIC WORDS
articles.summary: Vague urgency language leads to stress and miscommunication.
